checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the source document's content where present. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. Metadata titles and document headers are consistent with acceptable pipeline behavior.

The source document contains physical addresses but no phone numbers or email addresses. The physical address '301 North Wilmington Street, 7th Floor' is correctly transcribed. No fabricated contact information was found.

While significant text content is missing from within sections (see C5), the headings for all major sections (1-9) and sub-sections (6a-e, 8a-b) are present in the output. No entire pages or primary sections were omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a major failure in capturing the descriptive paragraphs within Section 6 and Section 8. The output lists the sub-section headings but entirely omits the body text following them. This includes the entirety of the reports for the Local Government Committee, State Government Users Group, Federal Interagency Committee (on Page 2), the Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee, Technical Advisory Committee, LiDAR Data, and Future M&O Meetings (on Page 3). Roughly 40% of the document's total text content is missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple missing text blocks including: 'The LGC last met on November 20th...'; 'The executive committee meeting was last week...'; 'There are two new members...'; 'Paul mentioned that the year 2022...'; 'Dan is stepping down...'; 'Hope reported that Esri...'; 'March 16, June 22...'
checks.b_evidence_output: The output for these sections contains only list items with the titles: 'Local Government Committee – Debbie Brannan', 'State Government Users Group Committee – John Farley', 'Federal Interagency Committee – Scott Lokken', 'd. Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee – Paul Badr', 'e. Technical Advisory Committee – Dan Madding', 'a. LiDAR Data', 'b. Future M&O Meetings'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 6 and 8, Pages 2 and 3, Element indices 24, 26, and 30.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted (Sections 1-5, Section 7) is accurate to the source. No wording changes altering meaning were detected in the converted portions.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not show any signs of duplicated content. Each section and paragraph from the source appears to be represented only once in the JSON structure.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Major sections 1 through 5 are treated as list items within individual list objects (elements 9, 11, 13, 15, 19), whereas major sections 6 through 9 are promoted to Heading Level 2 (elements 23, 27, 29, 31). This creates a fragmented and inconsistent navigational structure compared to the source where these sections are functionally equivalent.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Welcome, 2. Approval of Minutes..., 3. NC Parcels..., 4. Census..., 5. Statewide Orthoimagery..., 6. Status Reports..., 7. February 12, 2020..., 8. Other Items, 9. Adjourn
checks.b_evidence_output: Reading order 9, 11, 13, 15, 19 (lists) vs Reading order 23, 27, 29, 31 (H2 headings)

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables, and neither does the output. No structural corruption of tables is possible.

The structural reading order is fundamentally broken due to the total omission of body content for sub-sections. Specifically, the descriptive paragraphs for sections 6a through 6e (Local Government, State Government, Federal Interagency, Statewide Mapping, and Technical Advisory committees) and sections 8a and 8b (LiDAR Data and Future Meetings) are entirely missing. This results in an illogical flow where headings are provided without the information they are intended to introduce.
checks.b_evidence_source: Paragraphs beginning with: 'The LGC last met on November 20th...', 'The executive committee meeting...', 'There are two new members...', 'Paul mentioned that the year 2022...', 'Dan is stepping down...', 'Hope reported that Esri...', and 'March 16, June 22...'
checks.b_evidence_output: [The JSON contains list items for these sections at reading_order 24, 26, and 30, but no paragraph elements follow them.]

checks.a_analysis: The primary numbered list from the source (items 1-5) has been fragmented into five separate, single-item unordered lists. This breaks the structural relationship between these items. Additionally, the content belonging to the sub-lists in section 6 and 8 is missing as noted in S4.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1. Welcome
2. Approval of Minutes...
3. NC Parcels...
4. Census...
5. Statewide Orthoimagery...
checks.b_evidence_output: Reading order 9 (List item 'Welcome'), Reading order 11 (List item 'Approval...'), etc.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links present in the output. While there was a hyperlinked 'document' in the source (page 3, item 6e), the entire paragraph containing that link was omitted (as covered in S4). No fabricated links were injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the logical flow and visual organization of the source document. The header information, section sequence (Welcome through Adjourn), and hierarchical structure are preserved in a single-column layout suitable for HTML. While the PDF uses a numbered list (1-9) and the HTML uses a combination of bullets and headings for these sections, the relative position and grouping of information remain consistent with the source's intent.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present in the HTML output.
The source PDF is entirely text-based and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or charts. No images were expected or found in the HTML output.

A review of the HTML screenshot shows that no body content (paragraphs, list items, etc.) is duplicated. Section headings appear in both the Table of Contents sidebar and the main body, which is an expected and intentional accessibility feature for document navigation.
